TSLA: Tesla Is Quietly Becoming a Global Utility and Wall Street Barely Notices. Tesla's energy business, which generated nearly $12.8 billion in revenue in 2025, is rapidly growing and transforming the company into a global utility, yet Wall Street continues to primarily value it as a car manufacturer. With significant advancements in Megapack deployments, LFP battery production, and software-driven solutions like Autobidder and Virtual Power Plant, Tesla is building a comprehensive energy ecosystem. This shift is largely unrecognized by the market, which remains focused on automotive sales and delivery numbers.

Tesla's South Korean sales up more than 300% to 11,134 vehicles in March. Tesla's car registrations in South Korea surged by 330% to 11,134 vehicles in March compared to the previous year, according to market researcher Carisyou. This significant increase follows Tesla's recent price cuts for its Model Y and Model 3 electric vehicles manufactured in China, intensifying price competition among EV makers in Korea.

Rocket Lab, Beyond Meat, Micron Technology And More: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Buzzing About This Week. This article highlights five stocks—Tesla, Micron Technology, Rocket Lab, Nike, and Beyond Meat—that were trending among retail investors from March 30 to April 2 due to various factors like earnings reports, AI developments, and corporate news. Tesla missed delivery expectations, Micron faced volatility over an AI algorithm, Rocket Lab received regulatory approval for an acquisition, Nike plunged despite beating estimates, and Beyond Meat saw revenue decline amid weak demand.

SMOG ETF Volume Surges as Tesla, Rivian Diverge. The VanEck Low Carbon Energy ETF (SMOG) has experienced significantly higher trading volume than its daily average. This surge comes as two of its largest holdings, Tesla (TSLA) and Rivian (RIVN), show divergent performances and news. Tesla's stock is pressured after missing Q1 2026 delivery forecasts, while Rivian saw a 20% increase in deliveries, beat estimates, and secured a major investment and partnership with Uber.

Tesla Stock Keeps Falling - But 10% Lower TSLA Puts Yield 2.0% for One Month Out. Tesla Inc (TSLA) stock has been declining, driving up put option premiums. This presents an attractive opportunity for short-sellers and value investors. Using short out-of-the-money (OTM) puts allows investors to profit from the elevated premiums and potentially buy TSLA shares at a lower price point, with a 10% lower strike price put yielding 2.0% for one month out.

Weekend Round-Up: Tesla's Q1 Miss, Uber's Robotaxi Ambitions, Baidu's AV Safety Concerns, Ford's Bluecrui. This article summarizes a dynamic week in the tech and auto sectors, highlighting Tesla's missed Q1 delivery target and Uber's potential integration of Tesla Robotaxis. It also covers safety concerns regarding Baidu's Apollo Go Robotaxis, Ford's BlueCruise system being implicated in fatal collisions, and American Airlines' discussions with SpaceX for in-flight Wi-Fi.

Tesla Stock Keeps Falling - But 10% Lower TSLA Puts Yield 2.0% for One Month Out. Tesla (TSLA) stock has seen a recent decline, pushing up put option premiums. This situation allows investors to sell out-of-the-money (OTM) puts, achieving attractive yields while setting a lower potential buy-in point for the stock. Despite the recent dip, analysts still maintain significantly higher price targets for TSLA, suggesting potential upside for long-term investors.

Dad, Son Burned to Death in Tesla After Battery System Fire, Lawsuit Claims. A mother has filed a wrongful death lawsuit against Tesla after her teenage son and his father were burned to death in a fiery Tesla Model 3 crash in Georgia in December 2024. The lawsuit alleges that the car's doors malfunctioned, trapping the occupants, and claims design flaws in Tesla's Autopilot system and battery pack contributed to the incident. The complaint seeks damages for the son's suffering and death, along with legal fees and funeral expenses.

Eyes on AI! Wedbush Keeps $600 Tesla Stock Price Target despite Q1 Miss. Despite Tesla missing Wall Street's Q1 delivery estimates for the second consecutive quarter and falling short on energy storage deployment, Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintained his Buy rating and $600 price target for TSLA stock. Ives attributes his bullish outlook to Tesla's strong AI push and robotaxi plans, projecting significant future revenue from these areas. While acknowledging challenges in Europe, Ives noted strong growth in China and emphasized the company's long-term strategy centered on AI and robotics investments.

Sky Quarry, Sidus Space, Transocean, United Airlines And Tesla: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today. This article highlights five stocks—Sky Quarry, Sidus Space, Transocean, United Airlines, and Tesla—that captured investor attention. Sky Quarry and Sidus Space saw significant gains due to company-specific news regarding crude production and NASA ties, respectively. Transocean also rose on new drilling contracts and debt reduction, while United Airlines and Tesla experienced declines due to concerns over rising fuel costs and lower-than-expected delivery figures, respectively.

SMOG ETF Volume Surges 17x as Tesla Slumps. The VanEck Low Carbon Energy ETF (SMOG) experienced an unusual trading volume, surging 17 times its daily average. This surge coincides with Tesla's weaker-than-expected Q1 2026 deliveries, which pressured the stock and led analysts to cut price targets despite long-term AI and robotaxi ambitions. In contrast, Rivian, a smaller holding, reported strong delivery growth and received more optimistic analyst commentary due to its R2 platform and Uber partnership.

Tesla Rival Rivian's US Sales Decline For Fourth Consecutive Month Ahead Of R2 Launch. Rivian Automotive Inc.'s US sales have declined for the fourth consecutive month, with a significant year-over-year drop in the first quarter of 2026. This comes as the company prepares to launch its new R2 Crossover SUV, which is expected to boost sales, and as it signed a significant deal with Uber for R2 Robotaxis. Rivian also unveiled its Universal Hands Free assisted driving system, designed to compete with Tesla's FSD.

Tesla Rival Rivian's US Sales Decline For Fourth Consecutive Month Ahead Of R2 Launch. Rivian Automotive Inc. has experienced a consecutive four-month decline in U.S. sales, with Q1 2026 sales falling by over 26.5% year-over-year. This comes as the company prepares to launch its R2 Crossover SUV, which is expected to boost sales, with 20,000 to 25,000 units anticipated in 2026. Rivian also recently signed a $1.25 billion deal with Uber for R2 Robotaxis and unveiled its Universal Hands Free assisted driving system.

This distribution is payable on April 12, 2024, to shareholders of record as of April 10, 2024. The ex-dividend date for this distribution is April 10, 2024.

First Solar’s New U.S. Facility And Tesla Shift Utility Scale Solar Economics. First Solar (FSLR) plans a new 3.7GW U.S. finishing facility to reduce tariff exposure and align with domestic incentives, shifting production from Southeast Asia. Concurrently, Tesla is entering U.S. solar manufacturing equipment, potentially altering market dynamics. These developments could impact First Solar's cost structure, competitive position, and the broader utility-scale solar industry.

Bloomberg: Tesla’s AI dreams don’t change reality, cars still pay the bills. Bloomberg's Craig observed that Tesla's future ambitions, such as autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots, are still theoretical, while the company's core automotive business recently missed delivery expectations for Q1 2026. Tesla delivered 358,000 units against a projected 372,000, signaling a dependency on car sales despite significant investments in AI and R&D. The company's high valuation presumes success in these future ventures, but current market predictions show low confidence in their near-term realization, making Tesla vulnerable if automotive sales decline further.

Tesla Rival Rivian's US Sales Decline For Fourth Consecutive Month Ahead Of R2 Launch. Rivian Automotive Inc. experienced its fourth consecutive month of declining U.S. sales, with a 26.5% year-over-year drop in Q1 2026 sales, totaling 8,141 vehicles. This decline precedes the highly anticipated launch of its R2 Crossover SUV, which is expected to boost sales and is priced significantly lower than its current premium models. Rivian also has a $1.25 billion deal with Uber for R2 Robotaxis and is developing its Universal Hands Free assisted driving system to compete with Tesla's FSD.
TGT: Aberdeen Group plc Purchases 21,662 Shares of The Kroger Co. $KR. Aberdeen Group plc has increased its stake in The Kroger Co. by 5.5%, acquiring an additional 21,662 shares. This purchase brings their total holdings to 414,587 shares, representing approximately 0.07% ownership valued at $25.9 million. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for Kroger, with an average target price of $74.56, following a recent earnings beat and dividend announcement.

Aberdeen Group plc Boosts Holdings in Kimberly-Clark Corporation $KMB. Aberdeen Group plc increased its stake in Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) by 19.6% in the fourth quarter, now holding 287,841 shares valued at approximately $29.04 million. Kimberly-Clark recently raised its quarterly dividend to $1.28 per share, resulting in an annualized dividend of $5.12 and a yield of 5.3%. The company currently has an average analyst rating of "Hold" with a target price of $115.85.

What stores are open Easter Sunday? Are Walmart, Trader Joe's open or closed today? Here's a list. This article provides a list of major retailers and stores that are expected to be open or closed on Easter Sunday 2026, including Walmart and Trader Joe's. While many stores like Walmart and Trader Joe's will be open, others such as Target, Costco, and Sam's Club are likely to be closed. It also advises customers to call their local stores to confirm holiday hours.

Dollar Tree Now Stocks This Italian Olive Oil—and It’s the Cheapest We’ve Seen. Dollar Tree is now carrying Bertolli Extra Virgin Olive Oil in 8.45-ounce bottles for $3.75, making it significantly cheaper per ounce than at other major retailers like Kroger, Target, and Walmart. This new offering comes as Dollar Tree has raised its prices, allowing the store to stock higher-quality products at competitive prices. The article highlights the value of this deal for shoppers looking to save on cooking staples amidst rising grocery costs.

Florida Trust Wealth Management Co Grows Holdings in Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST. Florida Trust Wealth Management Co. increased its stake in Costco Wholesale Corporation by 2.5% during the fourth quarter, reflecting a positive sentiment from institutional investors who collectively own 68.48% of the company's stock. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $1,039.13, supported by Costco's strong financial performance, strategic expansions, and membership-driven growth. Despite some insider selling, the company continues to outperform with solid fundamentals, including a recent EPS and revenue beat.

Is Costco open or closed on Easter? Here's what to know. This article provides information on which grocery and retail stores will be open or closed on Easter Sunday, April 5, 2026. Costco, Sam's Club, Target, Aldi, and Publix are among the stores confirmed to be closed. However, many other grocery chains like Walmart, Kroger family stores, and Trader Joe's, as well as retailers like Home Depot and Sephora, will be open.

Target opens massive 55th N.J. location, bringing 280 jobs to the community. A new 150,000-square-foot Target store has opened in West Orange Plaza, marking its 55th location in New Jersey and creating approximately 280 jobs. The store offers various services including drive-up, in-store pickup, and same-day delivery, and is part of a larger redevelopment project at the shopping center that includes new leases with other businesses. The redevelopment aims to enhance shopping options for the Essex County community.

Target opens massive new West Orange location with 280 jobs. A new 150,000-square-foot Target store recently opened in West Orange Plaza, creating approximately 280 jobs and expanding shopping options for the Essex County community. This is Target's 55th location in New Jersey and includes features such as drive-up service, a CVS pharmacy, and a Starbucks cafe. The opening is part of a larger redevelopment effort by Levin Management Corp. at the plaza.

Is Walmart Open on Easter 2026? Details on Holiday Hours. Walmart stores will be open during normal business hours on Easter Sunday, April 5, 2026, with the exception of locations in Maine which close due to state law. Target, Costco, and Aldi will be closed, but other stores like BJ's Wholesale Club, Dollar General, Home Depot, Kroger, Trader Joe's, and Whole Foods will also be open with varying hours. Customers are advised to check local store hours for confirmation.

Perigon Wealth Management LLC Has $16.61 Million Stake in Walmart Inc. $WMT. Perigon Wealth Management LLC reduced its stake in Walmart Inc. by 14.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 25,446 shares but still holding 149,110 shares worth $16.61 million. Walmart reported strong Q4 earnings, beating estimates with $0.74 EPS and $190.66 billion in revenue, and set positive FY2027 guidance. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with a target price of $135.76.

Federal Realty: The King of Dividend Growth. Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) is highlighted as a top dividend growth stock, having increased its dividend for 58 consecutive years, making it the only REIT on the Dividend Kings list. The company's strong performance is attributed to its portfolio of strategically located strip malls and mixed-use properties, active asset management focused on redevelopment and strategic sales, and an attractive dividend yield of 4.2%. Analysts from Scotiabank and Truist have recently updated their price targets for FRT, maintaining positive to hold ratings.

Ulta Beauty Launches Erly Skincare To Test Minimalist Demand And Loyalty. Ulta Beauty is launching Erly Skincare, a dermatologist-founded brand, nationwide on April 19, in 750 stores and online. This move targets Gen Z and Gen Alpha with minimalist, hypoallergenic products, expanding Ulta's skincare offerings and testing demand for simpler routines. The launch also allows Ulta to gain early access to brand equity and potentially boost loyalty among younger shoppers.

Director at Target (NYSE: TGT) receives 1,791-share equity award. Target Corporation director Stephen B. Bratspies was granted an equity award of 1,791 shares of common stock, reported via a Form 4 filing. These shares were awarded at $0.00 per share as compensation under the company's 2020 Long-Term Incentive Plan, increasing his direct holdings to 1,791 shares. This transaction is categorized as a compensation grant rather than a market purchase, aligning the director's interests with long-term company performance.

Target Corp (TGT) director Stephen Bratspies files Form 3 insider report. Target Corp director Stephen B. Bratspies has filed a Form 3 insider ownership report for TGT, indicating his new role as a director. The filing shows no reported transactions, exercises, gifts, or other movements in Target securities. A Form 3 is an initial statement of beneficial ownership, meaning it reports his entry into the role and not any stock activity.
SBUX: Starbucks completes JV deal with Boyu Capital in China. Starbucks has finalized a joint venture deal with private equity firm Boyu Capital, giving Boyu Capital control of 60% of Starbucks China's retail operations. Starbucks will retain the remaining 40% shareholding while continuing to own and license its brand and intellectual property to the JV. This partnership aims to expand Starbucks' presence in China, with a long-term goal of increasing the network to 20,000 outlets.

Starbucks Pumpkin Spice Latte: The Seasonal Phenomenon Driving Consumer Staples Revenue in North America. Starbucks' Pumpkin Spice Latte (PSL) continues to be a major revenue driver, generating over $500 million annually in the U.S. alone and reinforcing brand loyalty. The 2026 iteration features subtle enhancements and extends into various formats, strategically boosting foot traffic and digital orders for Starbucks. The PSL also plays a significant role in investor confidence, influencing Starbucks' Q3/Q4 earnings and contributing to the company's defensive appeal within consumer staples.

Target opens massive new West Orange location with 280 jobs. The opening is part of a larger redevelopment effort by Levin Management Corp. at the plaza.

Starbucks confirms changes to tipping system at certain locations from July 1. Starbucks is implementing several changes starting July 1, including an expanded tipping system for customers using its mobile app or card payments. Additionally, the company is introducing a bonus program for workers, offering up to $1,200 annually, and shifting to weekly pay for all U.S. partners by August. These initiatives are expected to increase worker pay by 5-8% on average.

Jim Cramer Discusses Starbucks (SBUX) CEO’s Turnaround Algorithm. Jim Cramer has consistently highlighted Starbucks (SBUX) CEO Brian Niccol's turnaround strategy, which he sees as successful, particularly when compared to Nike. Cramer, whose charitable trust holds a significant position in Starbucks, notes improvements in customer service and employee rosters. Guggenheim also recently raised its price target for SBUX to $95, maintaining a Neutral rating, and increased its Q2 same-store sales growth estimate despite reducing earnings projections for future fiscal years.

Starbucks set to restart bargaining in April. Starbucks Workers United are set to restart contract negotiations with Starbucks in April after a nationwide strike that tapered off from December to February. The union submitted a comprehensive contract proposal including a $17 wage floor and annual raises, along with various protections for workers. This comes amidst ongoing unfair labor practice charges against Starbucks and shareholder activism regarding the company's labor relations.

Winners And Losers Of Q4: Wendy's (NASDAQ:WEN) Vs The Rest Of The Traditional Fast Food Stocks. The broader market experienced shifts from AI concerns to geopolitical risks with the US-Iran conflict dominating investor psychology.

Froman was granted 1,087.7 shares of Disney Common Stock at $96.96 per share as a stock award, increasing his direct holdings to 23,184 shares. The grant reflects a neutral impact on the company's sentiment but significantly adds to Froman's direct and indirect stock ownership.

Assessing Nike's Performance Against Competitors In Textiles, Apparel & Luxury Goods Industry. This article provides an in-depth analysis of Nike's performance against its competitors in the Textiles, Apparel & Luxury Goods industry. It evaluates Nike based on critical financial metrics such as P/E, P/B, P/S ratios, ROE, EBITDA, gross profit, and revenue growth. The analysis concludes that while Nike has strong profitability and cash flow (EBITDA, gross profit), it shows a premium valuation (high P/E, P/B), lower efficiency in utilizing equity (ROE), and slower revenue growth compared to its peers.

Nike Shifts Production From China While Stretching Dividend And Valuation. Nike is strategically reducing its reliance on Chinese manufacturing for US-bound footwear, aiming to mitigate tariff impacts and manage profitability pressures. Despite these operational shifts, the company is maintaining its 24-year dividend growth streak, even though payouts currently exceed earnings and free cash flow coverage is low. This move highlights Nike's focus on shareholder returns amidst broader business challenges and investor scrutiny over its guidance, margins, and China sales.
